text.title: go-project-structure Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for go-project-structure. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Default to cmd+internal. Add pkg/ only when you can name an external module that imports code from it. Add a second cmd/<name> only when a real second binary exists.
rule.rationale: pkg/ is routinely misused as a dumping ground. Once code is in pkg/ it is technically importable; moving it to internal/ later is a breaking change for external consumers.

rule.statement: Graceful shutdown must close DB pools, drain queue consumers, and cancel contexts — not just call srv.Shutdown. Every long-running goroutine needs a select on quit or ctx.Done.
rule.rationale: Agents stop at srv.Shutdown and leave background goroutines running. Under Kubernetes SIGTERM, this causes in-flight requests to be abandoned and queued messages to be reprocessed.

rule.statement: Use a typed config loader (envconfig, koanf, viper) rather than hand-rolled getEnv helpers. Hand-rolled helpers silently return defaults on type-parse failures.
rule.rationale: A getEnv that returns "" on missing PORT instead of an error lets the server start on the wrong port silently. Typed loaders fail loudly at startup.

rule.statement: Define interfaces at the consumer (the service), not the producer (the repository). The service file owns UserRepository interface; the repository package provides the concrete impl.
rule.rationale: This is idiomatic Go: the interface lives with the code that uses it, not the code that satisfies it. It avoids forcing all consumers to import the repository package just to get an interface.

rule.statement: Every long-running goroutine (queue consumer, ticker, watcher) must select on a quit channel or context cancellation. srv.Shutdown alone does not stop background goroutines.
rule.rationale: Agents generate worker loops without exit conditions. Under SIGTERM, srv.Shutdown returns immediately but the goroutine leaks, processing messages against a closed DB pool.

rule.statement: Dockerfile must include USER nobody:nobody and use a distroless or minimal base image for the runtime stage. Never run Go binaries as root in production containers.
rule.rationale: Agents emit Dockerfiles without USER directives, leaving the binary running as root — a container escape vulnerability.

rule.statement: Generate an end-to-end vertical slice for at least one resource to prove the layout works. The slice should span cmd/<name>/main.go → internal/handler → internal/service → internal/repository → internal/model with tests at each layer.
rule.rationale: Agents generate empty directories and skip the actual implementation. A working CRUD endpoint proves package boundaries are correct.
